learningObjectives.title: Understand freight order lifecycle
learningObjectives.description: Explain status progression from creation to settlement.

learningObjectives.title: Understand status management
learningObjectives.description: Describe status categories and operational visibility controls.

learningObjectives.title: Understand execution monitoring
learningObjectives.description: Clarify how timely status updates improve control and communication.

sections.content: Every Freight Order in SAP TM progresses through a predefined lifecycle from creation until freight settlement. Status Management enables transportation planners, dispatchers and managers to monitor the execution progress, identify delays and ensure every shipment reaches its destination successfully.

sections.title: Key Concept
sections.content: Status Management provides complete visibility into the execution progress of every Freight Order.

A shipment leaves Hyderabad at 9:00 AM. The carrier updates the Freight Order status to 'In Transit'. After successful delivery in Chennai, the status changes to 'Delivered' and finally to 'Completed', allowing freight settlement to begin.

sections.title: Best Practice
sections.content: Update Freight Order statuses immediately after operational events such as loading, departure and delivery to maintain accurate transportation visibility.

sections.title: Real Project Example
sections.content: A logistics company integrated GPS tracking with SAP TM. Freight Order statuses changed automatically based on vehicle location, reducing manual updates by more than 80%.

sections.title: Interview Tip
sections.content: Certification questions frequently ask for the Freight Order lifecycle. Remember the sequence: Created -> Planned -> Released -> In Transit -> Delivered -> Completed -> Settled.

sections.title: Common Mistake
sections.content: Completing a Freight Order does not automatically complete Freight Settlement. Settlement is a separate business process that begins after transportation execution.

items: Freight Orders progress through multiple execution statuses.
Status Management provides end-to-end shipment visibility.
Execution, Planning and Settlement statuses are maintained separately.
Accurate status updates improve customer communication.
Freight Settlement begins after transportation completion.
